statement.p: An old cookbook has a recipe for soup that uses 20 grams of flour for thickener. The recipe makes 2.75 gallons of soup, but you are adjusting it to make 1.5 gallons. How much flour should you use? Set up an equation to answer this question. There is no need to solve it.

solution.p: Let x be how many grams of flour we should use. The amount of flour is proportional to the amount of soup, so we can compare the ratios of flour to soup for our batch with the original recipe:
